title: Scroll.in - Latest News, In depth news, India news, Politics news, Indian Cinema, Indian sports, Culture, Video News
meta_description: Scroll.in is an independent website covering news, politics, sports, culture and everything in between. Get the latest news, reportage, analysis and commentary on all that matters in India and beyond.
text_content: Scroll.in - Latest News, In depth news, India news, Politics news, Indian Cinema, Indian sports, Culture, Video News Please Don't look Away AI is hollowing out independent media, stealing our readership and revenue. Only you can stop this bleeding. Support our work. Join Scroll today! Become A Member Cover Story In the spotlight Ramachandra Guha: Of the three leaders at the heart of West Asia conflict, who is the most evil? Ramachandra Guha · 9 hours ago Editor's Picks Top stories of the day See all View from Dubai: Leaving is not an option when this is your home Yesterday · 06:30 am Why a proposal to resettle Parsis in Iran in the 1940s never took off Yesterday · 11:30 am World Poetry Day: Passion, migration, and spiritual geography in the Urdu poet Iftikhar Arif’s poems Yesterday · 05:30 pm Members Only Best of Scroll India’s top beef exporter made its highest-ever donation to the BJP Why Adivasis displaced by one of India’s first thermal power plants depend on stolen electricity How British artists painted Indian birds they had never seen When a film disappears and no one asks why Arundhati Roy talks movies: ‘Every person who reads my book has their own film in their head’ All Stories News. Politics. Sports. Culture. Sunday book pick: In ‘The Cat’, a newly-married elderly couple takes an abiding vow of hatred Georges Simenon wrote ‘Le Chat’ in French in 1967. Ros Schwartz’s English translation was published in 2025. Sayari Debnath · 2 hours ago ‘Please don’t leave me,’ Hind Rajab pleaded. Why her voice needs to be heard The Indian censor board has denied clearance for Kaouther Ben Hania’s film, which recreates a war crime committed in Gaza. Nandini Ramnath · 3 hours ago Artist Chittaprosad’s 1950s depiction of a labour camp speaks to Mumbai’s fast-changing present By juxtaposing workers against the emerging skyline of the city, the image can disrupt the tension between two forms of urbanism. Nikhil Rao · 4 hours ago Trending See all Fiction: When Allauddin Khilji conquers Madurai, the city’s goddess Meenakshi flees to Kumari Mar 20, 2026 · 05:30 pm ‘Glorification of couplehood skews honest conversations about love’: Writer Arundhati Ghosh 7 hours ago ‘We, The People of India’: TM Krishna’s book is part-explainer, part wake-up call for the republic Yesterday · 07:30 am Ramachandra Guha: Of the three leaders at the heart of West Asia conflict, who is the most evil? 9 hours ago Why a proposal to resettle Parsis in Iran in the 1940s never took off Yesterday · 11:30 am When reality doesn’t matter anymore Conspiracy theories – aided by AI – are no longer acts of the fringe. Also alarming is the sheer number of people willing to reject or overlook reality. Nachiket Deuskar · 7 hours ago Eco India: Can data tech help Mumbai's fishers tide over climate uncertainty? The new Karanja Port gives Mumbai’s fishers a chance to experiment with data tech and fair trade practices. Scroll Staff · 7 hours ago ‘Glorification of couplehood skews honest conversations about love’: Writer Arundhati Ghosh An interview with the author of ‘All Our Loves: Journeys with Polyamory in India’.
